sections.title: Overview
sections.content: IV giving sets (administration sets) connect IV fluid bags to an IV cannula for fluid or medication delivery. Standard sets deliver approximately 20 drops/mL (adults); blood giving sets and microdrip sets are also used in prehospital care.

sections.title: Types of Giving Sets
sections.content:
**Standard (macrodrip) giving set:**
- Drop factor: 20 drops/mL
- Use: IV fluid resuscitation, most IV medications

**Microdrip (paediatric) giving set:**
- Drop factor: 60 drops/mL
- Use: precise fluid delivery, paediatric patients, infusions

**Blood administration set:**
- Built-in 170–200 micron filter
- Use: pRBC, FFP, platelets
- Primed with 0.9% NaCl only (not Hartmann's — calcium precipitates)

**Burette (Buretrol):**
- 100–150 mL graduated chamber
- Use: precise paediatric fluid/medication delivery

sections.title: Priming & Setup
sections.content:
1. Check fluid bag for clarity, expiry, and integrity; confirm with prescription

2. Remove giving set from packaging aseptically; close roller clamp

3. Spike the bag: remove bag port protector and spike cover; insert spike firmly with a twisting motion

4. Squeeze drip chamber until half full

5. Open roller clamp and prime tubing until fluid flows continuously (free of air bubbles)

6. Close roller clamp

7. Connect to IV cannula; set drip rate

8. Drip rate formula: drops/min = (Volume mL × drop factor) ÷ time in minutes

sections.title: Key Points
sections.content:
- Label all lines with drug name, concentration, date, and time
- Inspect for air in tubing before connecting
- Blood sets: use within 4 hours once opened; do not use with dextrose
- Gravity infusion rate is affected by bag height (higher = faster) and patient position
